comments.comment:
Atmospheric releases of sulphuric acid occur as the result of coal/heavy ì
fuel oil combustion for the generation of electricity.

Coal is used as a fuel to generate electricity.  The resulting flyash and ì
bottom ash contain trace quantities of metals.  The metals were originally ì
in the coal.  Ash is landfilled at a managed site which has received ì
operating approval from the pro

Flyash is used as a high quality substitute for Portland Cement in ì
concrete.  The reuse of this former waste product displaces the need to ì
consume Portland Cement resources and extends the operating life of ì
existing ash landfill sites.

p2Plan.noActivitiesComment: NSPI is fully compliant with regulations for emissions and management of materials related to its operations. In producing safe and reliable electricity at the lowest cost for Nova Scotians, NSPI considers efficiency of operations among other factors in achieving sustainable business solutions.
